architecture.summary: Dense left-to-right decoder-only Transformer language models (244M to 137B parameters) pre-trained on web documents, dialog data, Wikipedia, and code-containing websites. Models are evaluated in two regimes: few-shot prompting via in-context examples, and fine-tuning on small task-specific datasets. The system generates Python code token-by-token conditioned on natural language problem descriptions and optional input-output examples. Functional correctness is verified by executing generated code against test cases.
trained_components: Transformer language model (decoder-only, full parameters fine-tuned)

biblio.title: Program Synthesis with Large Language Models

claims.metric: fraction of problems solved by any sample
claims.statement: On MBPP, the largest models without finetuning can synthesize solutions to 59.6% of the problems using few-shot learning with a well-designed prompt
Fine-tuning on MBPP improves performance by about 10 percentage points across most model sizes
On MathQA-Python, the largest fine-tuned model achieves 83.8% accuracy
Synthesis performance scales log-linearly with model size across evaluated models from 244M to 137B parameters

Natural language feedback from a human halves the error rate compared to the model's initial prediction, improving few-shot performance from 30% without human feedback to 65% with four turns of dialog
Even the largest models are generally unable to predict the output of a program given a specific input, whether few-shot or with fine-tuning

claims.metric: percent of problems with >50% line overlap in pre-training data
claims.statement: Only 32 of 974 problems (3.3%) in MBPP had more than half of their lines matched in the pre-training data; 91.5% had only one or two lines of code overlapping with the training set

Few-shot performance is quite sensitive to the particular examples given in the prompt; one set of examples solves 60% of tasks while many other examples solve far fewer, and ensembling prompts boosts performance from 59.6% to 66.4%
